comment: Aesop backend configuration examples. DO NOT include secrets in the actual aesop.config.json; use environment variables instead.

examples.name: Claude Code (default)
examples.description: Default backend: runs aesop through Claude Code Workflow harness. No API key needed (Claude Code manages auth). Tier 1 verification.

examples.name: OpenAI Codex / Chat Completions
examples.description: Uses OpenAI's Chat Completions API. Set OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable before running. Tier 2 verification (validate all JSON, spot-check, repair budget).

examples.name: OpenAI-compatible (OpenRouter hosted)
examples.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint on OpenRouter (a model aggregator). Set OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable. Tier 2 verification (hosted strong model).

examples.name: OpenAI-compatible (local Ollama)
examples.description: Local/small open model via Ollama (no API key required, or dummy 'local-only' used). Tier 3 verification (validate all output, heavy spot-check, adversarial review).

examples.name: OpenAI-compatible (Together.AI)
examples.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint on Together AI. Set TOGETHER_API_KEY environment variable. Tier 2 verification (hosted model).

production_config.comment: Recommended production configuration: Claude Code with Haiku workers, Tier 1 verification. Drop this into aesop.config.json (removing comments) to use.

experimental_codex.comment: Experimental Codex backend. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY set. Tier 2 verification.

experimental_ollama.comment: Experimental local Ollama backend. Requires 'ollama serve' running on localhost:11434. No API key needed (dummy 'local-only' used). Tier 3 verification.
